skill.name: libreoffice-calc
skill.description: Create, edit, format, export, and inspect LibreOffice Calc spreadsheets through the UNO API with sessions, charts, validation, and patch workflows.
skill.summary: Automate LibreOffice Calc spreadsheet work through bundled Python and UNO helpers.
skill.author: dfk1352

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many patterns, but review found no evidence of malicious intent, prompt injection, network access, or data exfiltration. The high cryptography and system reconnaissance findings are false positives from UNO URLs, exception names, imports, and documentation examples. Confirmed risks are limited to launching LibreOffice locally, using temporary profile directories, and reading one environment variable for UNO module discovery.

low_findings.title: Local LibreOffice Process Launch
low_findings.description: The skill starts the detected LibreOffice soffice executable in headless mode through subprocess.Popen. Arguments are passed as a list with no shell and no user-controlled command string, so command injection was not found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct subprocess.Popen usage is present and semantically tied to legitimate LibreOffice automation. The command path is discovered locally and arguments are fixed options, which makes injection unlikely.

low_findings.title: Temporary LibreOffice Profile Directory
low_findings.description: The bridge creates a temporary LibreOffice profile directory for process isolation and removes it during cleanup. This is a filesystem side effect but uses standard temporary-directory APIs and explicit cleanup.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The tempfile and cleanup calls are clear and bounded to the LibreOffice profile lifecycle. No evidence of broad file deletion or hidden persistence was found.

low_findings.title: Environment-Controlled UNO Module Path
low_findings.description: The bridge reads LIBREOFFICE_PROGRAM_PATH and may add it to Python module search paths to find LibreOffice UNO bindings. This is a legitimate configuration feature, but users should only set it to trusted LibreOffice directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable access is confirmed and has a plausible import-path impact. Risk is low because it depends on the caller controlling the runtime environment and is documented for local LibreOffice setup.

content.value_statement: Manual spreadsheet updates can be slow and inconsistent. This skill lets Claude, Codex, and Claude Code automate Calc files with structured Python and UNO workflows.
actual_capabilities: Create new LibreOffice Calc spreadsheets at absolute file paths.
Read and write cells or rectangular ranges with typed values.
Apply formatting such as bold text, italic text, colors, fonts, and number formats.
Manage sheets, named ranges, data validation rules, and formula recalculation.
Create, update, and delete bar, line, pie, and scatter charts.
Export spreadsheets to PDF, XLSX, or CSV and create PNG area snapshots.
limitations: Requires LibreOffice and Python UNO support on the host system.
All spreadsheet paths must be absolute paths.
Coordinates are zero-based, unlike the visible Calc user interface.
Snapshot dimensions may differ slightly from requested pixel sizes.

use_cases.title: Build recurring finance reports
use_cases.description: Populate report workbooks, format totals, recalculate formulas, and export final files for review.

use_cases.title: Prepare validated operations sheets
use_cases.description: Create sheets with controlled input ranges, named ranges, and validation messages for repeatable team workflows.

use_cases.title: Generate spreadsheet deliverables from data
use_cases.description: Write tabular data into ODS files, add charts, and export alternate formats for downstream users.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple workbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Calc workbook at /tmp/monthly-summary.ods. Add a sheet named Summary, write headers for Month, Revenue, and Cost, then add three example rows.

prompt_templates.title: Format and export a report
prompt_templates.prompt: Open /tmp/monthly-summary.ods. Format the header row as bold, apply currency formatting to the revenue and cost columns, recalculate formulas, and export the workbook to PDF.

prompt_templates.title: Add validation and a chart
prompt_templates.prompt: In /tmp/sales.ods, add validation to the sales value range so entries must be positive numbers. Create a line chart titled Monthly Sales from the month and sales columns.

prompt_templates.title: Apply an atomic report update
prompt_templates.prompt: Update /tmp/quarterly-report.ods in atomic mode. Replace the data table, format the header, define a named range for totals, create a pie chart, and roll back if any step fails.

output_examples.input: Create a workbook, add a revenue table, and export it to PDF.
output: Workbook created at the requested absolute path.
Revenue table written to the Summary sheet.
Header formatting and currency formatting applied.
PDF export completed at the requested output path.

output_examples.input: Read one formula cell from a Calc report.
output: The cell contains a formula result.
The response identifies the value, formula text, type, and any formula error.

output_examples.input: Create a chart from a data range.
output: Chart created on the target sheet.
The selected data range was used as the chart source.
The chart title and placement were applied.

best_practices: Use absolute paths for every workbook, export file, and snapshot file.
Use session context managers so LibreOffice resources close cleanly.
Use atomic patch mode when related spreadsheet changes must succeed together.
anti_patterns: Do not use one-based Calc UI coordinates in API calls.
Do not treat a one-cell range as the same target type as a cell.
Do not set LIBREOFFICE_PROGRAM_PATH to an untrusted directory.

faq.question: Does this skill require LibreOffice?
faq.answer: Yes. LibreOffice must be installed with Python UNO support before the bundled helpers can open or create Calc files.

faq.question: Which spreadsheet formats can it export?
faq.answer: The core export helpers support PDF, XLSX, and CSV output formats.

faq.question: Can it work with existing spreadsheets?
faq.answer: Yes. It can open existing files, edit cells and ranges, manage sheets, update charts, and save changes.

faq.question: How are rows and columns numbered?
faq.answer: Rows and columns are zero-based. Row 0 and column 0 refer to the first visible row and column in Calc.

faq.question: Can it create charts?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports bar, line, pie, and scatter charts with configurable data ranges, anchors, sizes, and titles.

faq.question: Is it safe to run on sensitive spreadsheets?
faq.answer: The reviewed code does not show network access or exfiltration. It does launch LibreOffice locally and writes files at requested paths.
